data.q: What is the primary difference between a 'Bundler' (like Webpack) and a 'Task Runner' (like Gulp)?
o: Bundlers only work with CSS; Task Runners only work with JS
Bundlers traverse a dependency graph to package assets; Task Runners execute a series of independent automated steps
Task Runners are built into browsers; Bundlers are for servers
There is no difference; they are the same thing
data.e: A bundler starts from an entry point and builds a map of every required file. A task runner simply automates repetitive tasks like minification or unit testing in a linear fashion.

data.q: What is 'Tree Shaking' in the context of modern build tools?
o: A method for organizing folder structures
The process of removing unused code (dead code) from the final bundle during build
An algorithm for optimizing image file sizes
A way to restart the development server automatically
data.e: Tree shaking relies on the static structure of ES6 modules (`import`/`export`) to identify and discard code that is never actually called in the application.

data.q: Why did modern tools like Vite and Esbuild significantly improve build speeds compared to older tools?
o: They use cloud computing for every build
They are written in low-level languages like Go or Rust and leverage native 'unbundled' ESM for development
They skip the minification step entirely
They only support one version of JavaScript
data.e: By using Go (Esbuild) or Rust (SWC), these tools bypass the performance bottlenecks of Node.js-based transpilers. Vite further improves dev speed by serving modules as native ESM.

data.q: What is the role of 'Babel' in a typical build pipeline?
o: To manage database connections
To transpile modern JavaScript (ES6+) into older versions compatible with legacy browsers
To bundle multiple CSS files into one
To host the application on a server
data.e: Babel allows developers to use the latest language features while ensuring the code still runs in environments that don't yet support them.

o: Replacing hardware components while the server is running
Updating only the changed modules in a running application without a full page reload
A method for cooling down server CPUs
A security protocol for form submissions
data.e: HMR preserves the application state (like text in an input or a toggle) while allowing the developer to see code changes instantly.

o: It provides a visual map of the server architecture
It maps compressed/minified code back to the original source code for easier debugging
It shows the physical location of the user
It documents every API endpoint in the project
data.e: Source maps allow developers to debug production code in the browser's developer tools as if they were looking at the original, unminified files.

o: Filling a database with dummy data
Providing modern functionality on older browsers that do not natively support it
The process of adding padding to a CSS layout
A technique for optimizing memory usage
data.e: A polyfill is a piece of code (usually JavaScript on the Web) used to provide modern features in older browsers that do not natively support them.

o: A chart showing the team's productivity
A data structure representing the relationships and dependencies between all files in a project
A specialized database for storing React components
The CSS layout for the main navigation
data.e: Build tools use the module graph to determine the order in which files should be processed and bundled to ensure dependencies are resolved correctly.

data.q: What is 'Transpilation' vs 'Compilation' in web development?
o: They are exactly the same
Transpilation is source-to-source (e.g., TS to JS); Compilation is source-to-machine code
Transpilation is only for CSS; Compilation is only for JS
Compilation happens in the browser; Transpilation happens on the server
data.e: Transpilation transforms code from one high-level language to another (like TypeScript to JavaScript), whereas traditional compilation usually converts to a lower-level executable.

o: A progress bar for the user
A transformation applied to the source code of a module (e.g., converting SASS to CSS)
A tool for uploading files to a CDN
A script that restarts the server
data.e: Loaders allow Webpack to process non-JavaScript files (like images, CSS, or TypeScript) and include them in the dependency graph.

o: The folder where the developer writes original code
The folder containing the final, optimized, and ready-to-deploy assets
A temporary directory for cache only
A folder for project documentation
data.e: The distribution folder is the result of the build process. It contains the minified and bundled files that are actually served to the user.

data.q: What is 'Pre-bundling' in tools like Vite?
o: Bundling the app before the developer starts writing code
Using Esbuild to convert heavy CommonJS/UMD dependencies into ESM for faster browser loading
A method for compressing images during the build
Pre-loading every page of the website on the user's device
data.e: Vite pre-bundles dependencies using Esbuild to improve page load speed and handle modules that aren't natively provided as ESM.
